title: It's about time we learn Transformers..
summary: A video tutorial explaining how generative pretrained transformers work under the hood. Covers self-attention, multi-head attention, feed forward networks, positional embeddings, layer normalization, and residual connections as core transformer components.
writeup: This video is a visual tutorial on the architecture of generative pretrained transformers, walking step by step through the core components: self-attention, multi-head attention, feed-forward networks, layer normalization, and residual connections. It ties these mechanics to production models including Gemini, Opus, and Qwen, framing the breakdown as the shared machinery behind them. The takeaway is a component-level understanding of how a transformer processes text, from embeddings through attention and softmax to the feed-forward and normalization stages.
